rows.requirement: Identity linking uses OAuth 2.0 as the v1 auth mechanism.
rows.quote:
**This specification uses
[OAuth 2.0](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749){ target="_blank" }**
as the v1 auth mechanism.
rows.notes: Capability dev.ucp.common.identity_linking. Identity-linking existed in 2026-01-23 but 04-08 reworked it to capability-driven scopes / RFC 8414 discovery. Converted from needs-oauth 2026-07-03: the OAuth harness (checks/oauth_harness.py) drives the authorization-code+PKCE flow as the platform; graded in merchant_checks_04_08_oauth.py.

rows.requirement: Platforms MUST authenticate token-endpoint requests using a method advertised in the business's token_endpoint_auth_methods_supported metadata.
rows.quote:
**MUST** authenticate token endpoint requests using a method advertised in
    the business's `token_endpoint_auth_methods_supported` metadata
rows.notes: RFC 8414 metadata field. Reclassified manual 2026-07-03: binds the PLATFORM (request author), not the business — no merchant check can grade it (suite requester-bound policy; CAT-034 precedent). Token-endpoint auth method selection is the platform's request behavior. Platform-conformance exemption candidate.

rows.requirement: Confidential clients SHOULD prefer asymmetric token-endpoint auth methods (private_key_jwt or tls_client_auth) and MAY use client_secret_basic where supported.
rows.quote:
**Confidential clients** (server-side platforms that can protect a
        credential) **SHOULD** prefer asymmetric methods —
        `private_key_jwt`
rows.notes: MAY client_secret_basic where business supports it. Reclassified manual 2026-07-03: binds the PLATFORM (request author), not the business — no merchant check can grade it (suite requester-bound policy; CAT-034 precedent). Confidential-client method preference is the platform's choice. Platform-conformance exemption candidate.

rows.requirement: Public clients MUST use token-endpoint auth method 'none' and rely on PKCE with S256 as proof-of-possession of the authorization code.
rows.quote:
**Public clients** (native, desktop, browser-extension, and on-device
        agent runtimes per
        [RFC 8252 §8.5](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8252#section-8.5){ target="_blank" })
        **MUST** use `none` and rely on PKCE with `S256`
rows.notes: Capability-driven OAuth model new in 04-08 rework. Reclassified manual 2026-07-03: binds the PLATFORM (request author), not the business — no merchant check can grade it (suite requester-bound policy; CAT-034 precedent). Public-client method choice + PKCE use are the platform's request behavior. Platform-conformance exemption candidate.

rows.requirement: Public clients MUST NOT embed a client_secret.
rows.quote:
Public clients
        **MUST NOT** embed a `client_secret`.
rows.notes: Mirrored at Security Considerations L581. Reclassified manual 2026-07-03: binds the PLATFORM (request author), not the business — no merchant check can grade it (suite requester-bound policy; CAT-034 precedent). Not embedding a client_secret is a property of the platform's distributed binary. Platform-conformance exemption candidate.

rows.requirement: Platforms MUST select the strongest token-endpoint auth method offered by the business compatible with the platform's deployment model.
rows.quote:
Platforms **MUST** select the strongest method offered by the business
    that is compatible with the platform's deployment model.
rows.notes: Reclassified manual 2026-07-03: binds the PLATFORM (request author), not the business — no merchant check can grade it (suite requester-bound policy; CAT-034 precedent). Method-strength selection is the platform's negotiation behavior. Platform-conformance exemption candidate.

rows.requirement: Platforms MUST include user identity tokens in the HTTP Authorization header using the Bearer scheme.
rows.quote:
**MUST** include user identity tokens in the HTTP `Authorization` header
    using the Bearer scheme: `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>`
rows.notes: RFC 6750 §2.1. Reclassified manual 2026-07-03: binds the PLATFORM (request author), not the business — no merchant check can grade it (suite requester-bound policy; CAT-034 precedent). The Authorization: Bearer header is authored by the platform. Platform-conformance exemption candidate.

rows.requirement: Platforms MUST process WWW-Authenticate: Bearer challenges on 401 and 403 responses to user-authenticated operations.
rows.notes: Reclassified manual 2026-07-03: binds the PLATFORM (request author), not the business — no merchant check can grade it (suite requester-bound policy; CAT-034 precedent). Challenge processing is the platform's response handling. Platform-conformance exemption candidate.

rows.requirement: Platforms MUST extract the scope parameter (when present) from the WWW-Authenticate challenge to construct subsequent authorization requests.
rows.quote:
Platforms **MUST** extract the `scope` parameter (when present) to
    construct subsequent authorization requests
rows.notes: Platforms drive flow from structured scope param, not error_description. Reclassified manual 2026-07-03: binds the PLATFORM (request author), not the business — no merchant check can grade it (suite requester-bound policy; CAT-034 precedent). Extracting the scope param is the platform's response handling. Platform-conformance exemption candidate.

rows.requirement: Platforms MUST implement the OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow as the account linking mechanism.
rows.quote:
**MUST** implement the OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow
    ([RFC 6749 §4.1](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749#section-4.1){ target="_blank" })
    as the account linking mechanism.
rows.notes: Authorization Code flow is mandatory. Reclassified manual 2026-07-03: binds the PLATFORM (request author), not the business — no merchant check can grade it (suite requester-bound policy; CAT-034 precedent). The account-linking flow choice is the platform's client implementation. Platform-conformance exemption candidate.

rows.requirement: Platforms MUST use PKCE with code_challenge_method=S256 for all authorization code exchanges.
rows.quote:
**MUST** use PKCE
    ([RFC 7636](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7636){ target="_blank" })
    with `code_challenge_method=S256` for all authorization code exchanges.
rows.notes: PKCE S256 mandatory. Reclassified manual 2026-07-03: binds the PLATFORM (request author), not the business — no merchant check can grade it (suite requester-bound policy; CAT-034 precedent). PKCE use on the request side is the platform's; the business-side enforcement is IDL-019/023/036/049 (checked). Platform-conformance exemption candidate.

rows.requirement: Platforms MUST validate the iss parameter in the authorization response to prevent Mix-Up Attacks, verifying it matches the AS issuer URI; if it does not match they MUST abort and discard the authorization response.
rows.quote:
**MUST** validate the `iss` parameter in the authorization response
    ([RFC 9207](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9207){ target="_blank" })
    to prevent Mix-Up Attacks. The platform **MUST** verify that the `iss`
    value matches the authorization server's issuer URI ... If the values do not match, the platform **MUST**
    abort and discard the authorization response.
rows.notes: Mix-up defense. Issuer declared in RFC 8414 metadata. Reclassified manual 2026-07-03: binds the PLATFORM (request author), not the business — no merchant check can grade it (suite requester-bound policy; CAT-034 precedent). iss validation is the platform's response handling; the business-side iss emission is IDL-018 (checked). Platform-conformance exemption candidate.

rows.requirement: Platforms SHOULD include a unique unguessable state parameter in the authorization request to prevent CSRF.
rows.quote:
**SHOULD** include a unique, unguessable `state` parameter in the
    authorization request to prevent CSRF
rows.notes: Reclassified manual 2026-07-03: binds the PLATFORM (request author), not the business — no merchant check can grade it (suite requester-bound policy; CAT-034 precedent). The state parameter is authored by the platform. Platform-conformance exemption candidate.

rows.requirement: Platforms MUST call the business's token revocation endpoint when a user initiates an unlink action on the platform side.
rows.quote:
**MUST** call the business's token revocation endpoint
        ([RFC 7009](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7009){ target="_blank" })
        when a user initiates an unlink action on the platform side.
rows.notes: Restated at Security Considerations L617-L618. Reclassified manual 2026-07-03: binds the PLATFORM (request author), not the business — no merchant check can grade it (suite requester-bound policy; CAT-034 precedent). Calling the revocation endpoint on unlink is the platform's action; the business-side revocation endpoint is IDL-027/028 (checked). Platform-conformance exemption candidate.

rows.requirement: Businesses MUST implement OAuth 2.0.
rows.quote:
**MUST** implement OAuth 2.0
    ([RFC 6749](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749){ target="_blank" }).
rows.notes: Converted from needs-oauth 2026-07-03: the OAuth harness (checks/oauth_harness.py) drives the authorization-code+PKCE flow as the platform; graded in merchant_checks_04_08_oauth.py.

rows.requirement: Businesses MUST publish authorization server metadata via RFC 8414 at /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server.
rows.notes: RFC 8414 well-known metadata is a fetchable endpoint; RFC 8414 discovery is the 04-08 rework.

rows.requirement: Businesses MUST populate scopes_supported in RFC 8414 metadata so platforms can detect scope mismatches before initiating an authorization flow.
rows.quote:
**MUST** populate `scopes_supported` in
    [RFC 8414](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8414){ target="_blank" }
    metadata to allow platforms
    to detect scope mismatches before initiating an authorization flow.
rows.notes: Restated at Security Considerations L613-L614.

rows.requirement: Businesses MUST return the iss parameter in the authorization response.
rows.quote:
**MUST** return the `iss` parameter in the authorization response
    ([RFC 9207](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9207){ target="_blank" }).
rows.notes: Mix-up defense companion to IDL-012. Restated at L589-L590. Converted from needs-oauth 2026-07-03: the OAuth harness (checks/oauth_harness.py) drives the authorization-code+PKCE flow as the platform; graded in merchant_checks_04_08_oauth.py.

rows.requirement: Businesses MUST enforce PKCE validation at the token endpoint for all authorization code exchanges; requests without a valid code_verifier MUST be rejected.
rows.quote:
**MUST** enforce PKCE
    ([RFC 7636](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7636){ target="_blank" })
    validation at the token endpoint for all authorization code exchanges.
    Requests without a valid `code_verifier` **MUST** be rejected.
rows.notes: Converted from needs-oauth 2026-07-03: the OAuth harness (checks/oauth_harness.py) drives the authorization-code+PKCE flow as the platform; graded in merchant_checks_04_08_oauth.py.

rows.requirement: Businesses MUST enforce exact string matching for the redirect_uri parameter during authorization requests; the token-request redirect_uri MUST be identical to the authorization-request one.
rows.quote:
**MUST** enforce exact string matching for the `redirect_uri` parameter
    during authorization requests to prevent open redirects and token theft.
    The `redirect_uri` in the token request **MUST** be identical to the one
    in the authorization request.
rows.notes: Loopback exception (127.0.0.1 / [::1]): ignore port, match scheme/host/path. Restated at Security Considerations L602-L604. Converted from needs-oauth 2026-07-03: the OAuth harness (checks/oauth_harness.py) drives the authorization-code+PKCE flow as the platform; graded in merchant_checks_04_08_oauth.py.

rows.requirement: For loopback redirect URIs (127.0.0.1 or [::1]) businesses MUST ignore the port component and match on scheme, host, and path only.
rows.quote:
**Exception — loopback redirects:** For
    redirect URIs targeting `127.0.0.1` or `[::1]`, businesses **MUST** ignore
    the port component and match on scheme, host, and path only
rows.notes: RFC 8252 §7.3 native/desktop ephemeral ports. Converted from needs-oauth 2026-07-03: the OAuth harness (checks/oauth_harness.py) drives the authorization-code+PKCE flow as the platform; graded in merchant_checks_04_08_oauth.py.

rows.requirement: Businesses MUST declare supported client authentication methods in token_endpoint_auth_methods_supported and enforce one of the declared methods at the token endpoint.
rows.quote:
**MUST** declare supported client authentication methods in
    `token_endpoint_auth_methods_supported`
    ([RFC 8414](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8414){ target="_blank" }) and enforce one of the declared methods at the token endpoint.
rows.notes: Presence in RFC 8414 metadata is fetch-testable; enforcement is needs-oauth.

rows.requirement: When 'none' is advertised, businesses MUST require PKCE with S256 and MUST reject any authorization code redemption lacking a valid code_verifier.
rows.quote:
When `none` is advertised, businesses **MUST** require PKCE with `S256` and
    **MUST** reject any authorization code redemption that lacks a valid
    `code_verifier`.
rows.notes: Public-client path. Converted from needs-oauth 2026-07-03: the OAuth harness (checks/oauth_harness.py) drives the authorization-code+PKCE flow as the platform; graded in merchant_checks_04_08_oauth.py.

rows.requirement: Requests that fail the negotiated authentication method MUST be rejected with invalid_client; requests that fail PKCE MUST be rejected with invalid_grant.
rows.quote:
Requests that fail the negotiated authentication method
    **MUST** be rejected with `invalid_client`; requests that fail PKCE
    **MUST** be rejected with `invalid_grant`.
rows.notes: Converted from needs-oauth 2026-07-03: the OAuth harness (checks/oauth_harness.py) drives the authorization-code+PKCE flow as the platform; graded in merchant_checks_04_08_oauth.py.

rows.requirement: Businesses MUST validate user identity tokens on every user-authenticated request (iss, aud, exp, scopes, and client_id/azp).
rows.quote:
**MUST** validate user identity tokens on every user-authenticated request:
    verify `iss`, `aud` (the business's resource server identifier), `exp`,
    scopes, and `client_id` / `azp` (or equivalent) to confirm the token was
    issued to the authenticated platform client
rows.notes: RFC 9068 §4. Converted from needs-oauth 2026-07-03: the OAuth harness (checks/oauth_harness.py) drives the authorization-code+PKCE flow as the platform; graded in merchant_checks_04_08_oauth.py. Graded on its observable core: an unverifiable/tampered Bearer token on a user-authenticated request is rejected with the invalid_token challenge; whether validation specifically inspects iss/aud/exp/azp is not black-box distinguishable. W2-F5 (2026-07-03): the identity.invalid_token_challenge check grades only the observable garbage-token core, which is NOT the full RFC 9068 claims validation this row demands (iss/aud/exp/azp on every request) — citation dropped rather than counting a partially-verified row. W3 (2026-07-03): the fixture now mints deterministic expired/revoked tokens (POST /testing/oauth/mint, config identity.token_mint) and identity.expired_token_rejected / identity.revoked_token_rejected (citing IDL-042 — its quote names 'expired') confirm the business validates exp + revocation on EVERY request; the --oauth-accept-any-token mutant proves those checks. That strengthens IDL-042's observable subset but does NOT close IDL-025: for an OPAQUE-token business the token carries no inspectable iss/aud/azp claims, and a resource request presents only the Bearer token (no client identity to bind), so aud (resource-server id) and client_id/azp validation are NOT black-box distinguishable. Kept needs-oauth rather than counting a partially-verified row (W2-F5 precedent). A reviewer MAY reclassify to manual (partial: exp/revocation/unverifiable observable; aud/azp irreducible for opaque tokens).

rows.requirement: Businesses MUST emit a WWW-Authenticate: Bearer challenge on 401 Unauthorized (identity_required) and 403 Forbidden (insufficient_scope) responses to user-authenticated operations.
rows.quote:
**MUST** emit a `WWW-Authenticate: Bearer` challenge per
    [RFC 6750 §3](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6750#section-3){ target="_blank" }
    on `401 Unauthorized` (`identity_required`) and `403 Forbidden`
    (`insufficient_scope`) responses to user-authenticated operations.
rows.notes: Restated at Security Considerations L593-L595. Converted from needs-oauth 2026-07-03: the OAuth harness (checks/oauth_harness.py) drives the authorization-code+PKCE flow as the platform; graded in merchant_checks_04_08_oauth.py.

rows.requirement: Businesses MUST implement token revocation; revoking a refresh_token MUST also immediately invalidate all access_tokens issued from it.
rows.quote:
**MUST** implement token revocation
    ([RFC 7009](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7009){ target="_blank" }).
    Revoking a `refresh_token` **MUST** also immediately invalidate all
    `access_token`s issued from it.
rows.notes: Converted from needs-oauth 2026-07-03: the OAuth harness (checks/oauth_harness.py) drives the authorization-code+PKCE flow as the platform; graded in merchant_checks_04_08_oauth.py.

rows.requirement: Businesses MUST support revocation requests authenticated with the same client credentials used at the token endpoint.
rows.quote:
**MUST** support revocation requests authenticated with the same client
    credentials used at the token endpoint.
rows.notes: Converted from needs-oauth 2026-07-03: the OAuth harness (checks/oauth_harness.py) drives the authorization-code+PKCE flow as the platform; graded in merchant_checks_04_08_oauth.py.

rows.requirement: Businesses MUST support standard UCP scopes as defined in the Scopes section.
rows.quote:
**MUST** support standard UCP scopes as defined in the
    [Scopes](#scopes) section.
rows.notes: Scope vocabulary: *.catalog.*:read, checkout:manage, order:read, order:manage. Capability-driven scopes new in 04-08 rework. Converted from needs-oauth 2026-07-03: the OAuth harness (checks/oauth_harness.py) drives the authorization-code+PKCE flow as the platform; graded in merchant_checks_04_08_oauth.py.

rows.requirement: Businesses SHOULD publish protected resource metadata at /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource and reference it via the resource_metadata parameter in WWW-Authenticate challenges.
rows.quote:
**SHOULD** publish protected resource metadata at
    `/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource`
    ([RFC 9728](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9728){ target="_blank" })
    and reference it via the `resource_metadata` parameter in
    `WWW-Authenticate` challenges.

rows.requirement: On RFC 8414 discovery, any non-2xx response other than 404 (network error or timeout) MUST abort and MUST NOT proceed to OIDC fallback.
rows.quote:
Any other non-2xx response, network error, or timeout: **MUST** abort.
      **MUST NOT** proceed to step 2.
rows.notes: Strict two-tier discovery hierarchy; RFC 8414 primary. Reclassified manual 2026-07-03: binds the PLATFORM (request author), not the business — no merchant check can grade it (suite requester-bound policy; CAT-034 precedent). Discovery abort semantics are the platform's fetch behavior. Platform-conformance exemption candidate.

rows.requirement: Platforms MUST NOT silently fall through on any error other than 404 in RFC 8414 discovery.
rows.quote:
Platforms **MUST NOT** silently fall through on any error other than `404` in
step 1.
rows.notes: Reclassified manual 2026-07-03: binds the PLATFORM (request author), not the business — no merchant check can grade it (suite requester-bound policy; CAT-034 precedent). Discovery fall-through is the platform's fetch behavior. Platform-conformance exemption candidate.

rows.requirement: The issuer value in discovered metadata MUST match the discovery base URI exactly; platforms MUST NOT normalize before comparison (byte-for-byte match).
rows.quote:
The `issuer` value in the discovered metadata **MUST** match the discovery
base URI exactly ... Platforms **MUST NOT** normalize (e.g., strip trailing slashes) before
comparison — the value must be a byte-for-byte match.
rows.notes: RFC 8414 §3.3. Restated at Security Considerations L605-L607. Converted from needs-oauth 2026-07-03: the OAuth harness (checks/oauth_harness.py) drives the authorization-code+PKCE flow as the platform; graded in merchant_checks_04_08_oauth.py. Business half graded (metadata issuer == discovery base, byte-for-byte); the 'platforms MUST NOT normalize' half is platform-bound.

rows.requirement: Platforms MUST request only the derived scope set (from config.scopes) and not a superset.
rows.quote: Platforms **MUST** request only the derived scope set — not a superset.
rows.notes: Derived from business config.scopes. Reclassified manual 2026-07-03: binds the PLATFORM (request author), not the business — no merchant check can grade it (suite requester-bound policy; CAT-034 precedent). Requesting only the derived scope set is the platform's request authoring. Platform-conformance exemption candidate.

rows.requirement: On the authorization response, the platform MUST verify state matches the sent value and iss matches the discovered issuer URI; if either fails it MUST discard the response.
rows.quote:
the platform **MUST** verify that the `state`
parameter matches the value sent in step 2, and that the `iss` parameter
matches the authorization server's `issuer` URI from discovered metadata.
If either check fails, the platform **MUST** discard the authorization
response.
rows.notes: Reclassified manual 2026-07-03: binds the PLATFORM (request author), not the business — no merchant check can grade it (suite requester-bound policy; CAT-034 precedent). state/iss verification is the platform's response handling. Platform-conformance exemption candidate.

rows.requirement: The token-request code_verifier MUST correspond to the code_challenge sent in the authorization request; businesses MUST reject token requests where code_verifier is absent or does not verify.
rows.quote:
The `code_verifier` **MUST** correspond to the
`code_challenge` sent in step 2. Businesses **MUST** reject token requests
where `code_verifier` is absent or does not verify against the stored
`code_challenge`.
rows.notes: Converted from needs-oauth 2026-07-03: the OAuth harness (checks/oauth_harness.py) drives the authorization-code+PKCE flow as the platform; graded in merchant_checks_04_08_oauth.py. Business half graded (token requests with an absent/non-verifying code_verifier are rejected); the platform half (send the corresponding verifier) is requester-bound.

rows.requirement: Scope names MUST match the pattern ^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$.
rows.quote: Scope names **MUST** match the pattern `^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$`.
rows.notes: Full scope_token pattern enforced in schema identity_linking.json#L26 (^[a-z][a-z0-9]*(?:\.[a-z][a-z0-9_]*)+:[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$). Scope vocabulary: {capability}:{scope} e.g. *.catalog.*:read, checkout:manage, order:read, order:manage.

rows.requirement: Platforms MUST treat any scope listed in config.scopes (well-known or custom) as gating its operations behind user authentication.
rows.quote:
Platforms **MUST** treat any
scope listed in `config.scopes` — well-known or custom — as gating its
operations behind user authentication.
rows.notes: config.scopes hard-gates user-auth operations vs scopes_supported accepted vocabulary. Reclassified manual 2026-07-03: binds the PLATFORM (request author), not the business — no merchant check can grade it (suite requester-bound policy; CAT-034 precedent). Treating config.scopes as gating is the platform's interpretation duty. Platform-conformance exemption candidate.

rows.requirement: Platforms MUST ignore unrecognized fields in a per-scope policy object.
rows.quote:
Platforms **MUST** ignore
unrecognized fields.
rows.notes: scope_policy is additionalProperties:true in schema. Reclassified manual 2026-07-03: binds the PLATFORM (request author), not the business — no merchant check can grade it (suite requester-bound policy; CAT-034 precedent). Ignoring unrecognized per-scope policy fields is the platform's parsing duty. Platform-conformance exemption candidate.

rows.requirement: On identity_required the business MUST return 401 Unauthorized, a WWW-Authenticate: Bearer challenge, and a UCP error body with code 'identity_required'.
rows.quote:
the business **MUST** return:

* HTTP `401 Unauthorized`
* A `WWW-Authenticate: Bearer` challenge header per
    [RFC 6750 §3](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6750#section-3){ target="_blank" }
* A UCP error response body containing a message with
    `code: "identity_required"`
rows.notes: Converted from needs-oauth 2026-07-03: the OAuth harness (checks/oauth_harness.py) drives the authorization-code+PKCE flow as the platform; graded in merchant_checks_04_08_oauth.py.

rows.requirement: The identity_required WWW-Authenticate header MUST include a realm parameter set to the business's issuer URI from RFC 8414 metadata.
rows.quote:
The `WWW-Authenticate` header **MUST** include a `realm` parameter set to
the business's issuer URI as declared in
rows.notes: realm MUST match issuer URI; see L600-L601. Converted from needs-oauth 2026-07-03: the OAuth harness (checks/oauth_harness.py) drives the authorization-code+PKCE flow as the platform; graded in merchant_checks_04_08_oauth.py.

rows.requirement: When the request included a token that was invalid/expired/unverifiable, the identity_required header MUST also include error="invalid_token".
rows.quote:
When the request included a token but it was invalid, expired, or
unverifiable, the header **MUST** also include `error="invalid_token"`
rows.notes: When no token presented, error SHOULD be omitted (RFC 6750 §3.1). Converted from needs-oauth 2026-07-03: the OAuth harness (checks/oauth_harness.py) drives the authorization-code+PKCE flow as the platform; graded in merchant_checks_04_08_oauth.py.

rows.requirement: The identity_required WWW-Authenticate header SHOULD include a resource_metadata parameter pointing to the protected resource metadata document.
rows.quote:
The header **SHOULD** include a `resource_metadata` parameter
([RFC 9728 §5.1](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9728#section-5.1){ target="_blank" })
pointing to the protected resource metadata document
rows.notes: Converted from needs-oauth 2026-07-03: the OAuth harness (checks/oauth_harness.py) drives the authorization-code+PKCE flow as the platform; graded in merchant_checks_04_08_oauth.py.

rows.requirement: continue_url MUST NOT be used to convey a pre-baked OAuth authorization request; the platform constructs its own authorization request.
rows.quote:
`continue_url`
**MUST NOT** be used to convey a pre-baked OAuth
authorization request; the platform constructs its own authorization
request from the `WWW-Authenticate` challenge and discovered metadata
rows.notes: Converted from needs-oauth 2026-07-03: the OAuth harness (checks/oauth_harness.py) drives the authorization-code+PKCE flow as the platform; graded in merchant_checks_04_08_oauth.py.

rows.requirement: On insufficient_scope the business MUST return 403 Forbidden, a WWW-Authenticate: Bearer challenge, and a UCP error body with code 'insufficient_scope'.
rows.quote:
the business **MUST** return:

* HTTP `403 Forbidden`
* A `WWW-Authenticate: Bearer` challenge header per RFC 6750 §3
* A UCP error response body containing a message with
    `code: "insufficient_scope"`
rows.notes: Converted from needs-oauth 2026-07-03: the OAuth harness (checks/oauth_harness.py) drives the authorization-code+PKCE flow as the platform; graded in merchant_checks_04_08_oauth.py.

rows.requirement: The insufficient_scope WWW-Authenticate header MUST include realm, error="insufficient_scope", and scope="<space-separated full required scope set>".
rows.quote:
The `WWW-Authenticate` header **MUST** include:

* `realm="<business issuer URI>"`
* `error="insufficient_scope"`
* `scope="<space-separated full required scope set for the operation>"`
rows.notes: Converted from needs-oauth 2026-07-03: the OAuth harness (checks/oauth_harness.py) drives the authorization-code+PKCE flow as the platform; graded in merchant_checks_04_08_oauth.py.

rows.requirement: The insufficient_scope scope parameter MUST list the full set of scopes required for the operation, not just the missing ones.
rows.quote:
The `scope` parameter **MUST** list the **full** set of scopes required
for the operation, not just the missing ones.
rows.notes: Enables incremental authorization. Converted from needs-oauth 2026-07-03: the OAuth harness (checks/oauth_harness.py) drives the authorization-code+PKCE flow as the platform; graded in merchant_checks_04_08_oauth.py.

rows.requirement: Platforms MUST NOT retry an insufficient_scope response by re-initiating a fresh account linking flow; they MUST instead request only the missing scope(s) via incremental authorization.
rows.quote:
Platforms **MUST NOT** retry an `insufficient_scope` response by
> re-initiating a fresh account linking flow — they **MUST** instead request
> only the missing scope(s) via incremental authorization to preserve
> previously granted scopes.
rows.notes: Reclassified manual 2026-07-03: binds the PLATFORM (request author), not the business — no merchant check can grade it (suite requester-bound policy; CAT-034 precedent). Incremental-authorization retry behavior is the platform's flow control. Platform-conformance exemption candidate.

rows.requirement: Plain PKCE (plain) MUST NOT be used; PKCE S256 is REQUIRED for all authorization code flows, and businesses MUST reject exchanges without a valid code_verifier.
rows.quote:
PKCE (`S256`) is REQUIRED for all authorization code flows.
  Plain PKCE (`plain`) **MUST NOT** be used. Businesses **MUST** reject
  authorization code exchanges without a valid `code_verifier`.
rows.notes: Security Considerations. Converted from needs-oauth 2026-07-03: the OAuth harness (checks/oauth_harness.py) drives the authorization-code+PKCE flow as the platform; graded in merchant_checks_04_08_oauth.py.

rows.requirement: Public clients cannot keep a client_secret confidential and MUST use 'none'; businesses MUST NOT require client_secret_basic as the only method when serving native or agent platforms.
rows.quote:
Public clients (native, desktop, and
  on-device agents per
  [RFC 8252 §8.5](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8252#section-8.5){ target="_blank" })
  cannot keep a `client_secret` confidential and **MUST** use `none`... Businesses **MUST NOT** require
  `client_secret_basic` as the only method when serving native or agent
  platforms.
rows.notes: Converted from needs-oauth 2026-07-03: the OAuth harness (checks/oauth_harness.py) drives the authorization-code+PKCE flow as the platform; graded in merchant_checks_04_08_oauth.py.

rows.requirement: Platforms MUST process the structured scope and error parameters to drive authorization flow decisions; error_description MUST NOT be used for control-flow decisions.
rows.quote:
Platforms
  **MUST** process the structured `scope` and `error` parameters to drive
  authorization flow decisions; `error_description` is a human-readable
  hint only and **MUST NOT** be used for control-flow decisions.
rows.notes: Platforms drive flow from structured scope/error params, not error_description. Reclassified manual 2026-07-03: binds the PLATFORM (request author), not the business — no merchant check can grade it (suite requester-bound policy; CAT-034 precedent). Driving flow decisions from structured params is the platform's logic. Platform-conformance exemption candidate.

rows.requirement: The realm parameter MUST match the business's issuer URI so platforms can correlate the challenge with the correct authorization server.
rows.quote:
The
  `realm` parameter **MUST** match the business's issuer URI so platforms
  can correlate the challenge with the correct authorization server.
rows.notes: Converted from needs-oauth 2026-07-03: the OAuth harness (checks/oauth_harness.py) drives the authorization-code+PKCE flow as the platform; graded in merchant_checks_04_08_oauth.py.

rows.requirement: All communication between platform and business MUST use HTTPS with a minimum of TLS 1.2.
rows.quote:
All communication between platform and business
  **MUST** use HTTPS with a minimum of TLS 1.2
rows.notes: TLS version assertion requires CLI/transport-level inspection, not browser fetch.

rows.requirement: Platforms SHOULD verify the derived scope set is a subset of scopes_supported before initiating the authorization flow.
rows.quote:
Platforms **SHOULD** verify that the derived scope set is
  a subset of `scopes_supported` before initiating the authorization flow, to
  fail fast on scope mismatches rather than at the consent screen.
rows.notes: config.scopes vs scopes_supported relationship. Reclassified manual 2026-07-03: binds the PLATFORM (request author), not the business — no merchant check can grade it (suite requester-bound policy; CAT-034 precedent). The scopes_supported subset pre-check is the platform's; the business-side scopes_supported publication is IDL-017 (checked). Platform-conformance exemption candidate.

rows.requirement: Platforms MUST revoke user identity tokens at the business's revocation endpoint when a user unlinks; businesses MUST reject subsequent requests that present revoked tokens.
rows.quote:
Platforms **MUST** revoke user identity tokens at the
  business's revocation endpoint (RFC 7009) when a user unlinks their account.
  Businesses **MUST** reject subsequent requests that present revoked tokens.
rows.notes: Converted from needs-oauth 2026-07-03: the OAuth harness (checks/oauth_harness.py) drives the authorization-code+PKCE flow as the platform; graded in merchant_checks_04_08_oauth.py. Business half graded (revoked tokens are rejected after RFC 7009 revocation); the 'platforms MUST revoke on unlink' half is platform-bound.

rows.requirement: When config.providers is absent (v1), platforms MUST fall back to direct OAuth 2.0 against the business domain via RFC 8414 discovery.
rows.quote:
When `config.providers` is
absent (as in v1), platforms **MUST** fall back to direct OAuth 2.0 against the
business domain via RFC 8414 discovery.
rows.notes: Forward-compat. config.providers is a reserved extension point in schema $comment. Reclassified manual 2026-07-03: binds the PLATFORM (request author), not the business — no merchant check can grade it (suite requester-bound policy; CAT-034 precedent). The v1 fallback is the platform's flow selection. Platform-conformance exemption candidate.

rows.requirement: When config contains fields not defined in this version, platforms MUST ignore those fields and proceed using OAuth 2.0 with RFC 8414 discovery on the business domain.
rows.quote:
When `config` contains fields
not defined in this version of the spec (`providers` or any other future field),
platforms **MUST** ignore those fields and proceed using OAuth 2.0 with RFC 8414
discovery on the business domain, as defined here.
rows.notes: Reclassified manual 2026-07-03: binds the PLATFORM (request author), not the business — no merchant check can grade it (suite requester-bound policy; CAT-034 precedent). Ignoring future config fields is the platform's parsing duty. Platform-conformance exemption candidate.

rows.requirement: authorization_response_iss_parameter_supported and code_challenge_methods_supported:["S256"] MUST both be present in UCP-compliant RFC 8414 metadata.
rows.quote:
`code_challenge_methods_supported: ["S256"]` signals PKCE.
Both **MUST** be present in UCP-compliant metadata.
rows.notes: authorization_response_iss_parameter_supported:true advertises RFC 9207; fetchable from /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server.

rows.requirement: config.scopes property names MUST conform to the scope_token pattern {capability}:{scope}.
rows.notes: scope_token $def applied as propertyNames of config.scopes (schema L54). Capability-driven scope vocabulary new in 04-08 rework.

rows.requirement: Business identity_linking config object is required and MUST contain a 'scopes' property.
rows.quote: "required": ["config"],..."required": ["scopes"],
rows.notes: business_schema requires config; config requires scopes (config.scopes). additionalProperties:true keeps config open for the reserved providers extension point.

notes: No official conformance oracle exists for identity-linking: .vendor/conformance contains only checkout/order/fulfillment/card-credential/webhook/etc. tests; grep found no identity-linking, oauth, or scope tests. All rows official_oracle=false.

Identity linking EXISTED in the 2026-01-23 version, but the 2026-04-08 version REWORKED it onto capability-driven scopes ({capability}:{scope}) and RFC 8414 authorization-server discovery. This is the spec-significant delta; flagged on rows IDL-001, IDL-016, IDL-029, IDL-037, IDL-059.

Almost all normative rows are OAuth-flow obligations requiring a live authorization server / browser-driven consent, hence testability=needs-oauth. The fetch-only obligations (RFC 8414 metadata presence: scopes_supported, token_endpoint_auth_methods_supported, code_challenge_methods_supported, authorization_response_iss_parameter_supported, protected-resource metadata, well-known publication) are marked testable. TLS-1.2-minimum (IDL-053) is testable but not browser_capable (transport-level inspection).

schema_enforced=true only for real JSON-Schema constraints in identity_linking.json: scope_token regex pattern (IDL-037/IDL-059) and the required config/scopes structure (IDL-060). The scope-name pattern ^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$ in prose (IDL-037) is a subset of the full schema scope_token pattern.

Scope vocabulary referenced across rows: *.catalog.*:read, dev.ucp.shopping.checkout:manage, dev.ucp.shopping.order:read, dev.ucp.shopping.order:manage (well-known); businesses MAY declare custom scopes following the same {capability}:{scope} convention.

config.scopes (UCP hard gates: scopes that REQUIRE user auth) vs OAuth scopes_supported (accepted vocabulary the AS will honor) are distinct; their set difference is the optional/auth-unlocked layer (IDL-017, IDL-038, IDL-054).
